title: Fix save/load diagram persistence and restrict icon drag to image only

requirements.text: Fix the save diagram functionality so that when a user saves a diagram, it is correctly persisted to the backend and reappears when the diagram list (loadout/sidebar) is opened or refreshed. The saved diagram should appear in DiagramSidebar and LoadDiagramDialog after saving without requiring a page reload.

quotes: fix the save diagram and appear on loadout

acceptanceCriteria: After saving a diagram via SaveDiagramDialog, the diagram appears in DiagramSidebar and LoadDiagramDialog immediately without a page reload.
Loading a saved diagram from the sidebar/dialog restores all icons, connections, drawings, lines, text labels, and images correctly.
The useSaveDiagramState mutation correctly invalidates the allDiagrams and diagramState caches on success so the list refreshes.
No data is lost between save and load operations.

requirements.text: In the AttackPathToolbar, restrict drag-and-drop behavior on icon buttons so that only the icon image element is draggable, not the text label beneath it. The draggable attribute and drag event handlers (onDragStart) should be scoped to the icon image element only, not the entire button or label text. The selected element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/main[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[7]/button[3] is one such toolbar icon button.

quotes: only drag the icon image not the label

acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a toolbar icon button initiates the drag using only the icon image as the drag source/ghost, not the label text.
The label text beneath the icon is not draggable and does not appear in the drag ghost image.
Dropping the dragged icon onto the canvas places the correct icon type at the drop position.
All toolbar icon buttons exhibit this behavior consistently, not just the third button in div[7].

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve all existing diagram state types and backend data models.
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or layout of the toolbar.
Changing authentication or user profile logic.
